# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

require 'action_dispatch/session/abstract_store'
require 'base64'
require 'securerandom'
require 'rack/request'

class MarshalCookieStore < ActionDispatch::Session::AbstractStore
  # ActionDispatch::Session::AbstractStore expects the session ID object to
  # respond to #to_session_digest for certain internal checks.
  # This custom class ensures compatibility.
  class SessionId < String
    def to_session_digest
      self
    end
  end

  def initialize(app, options = {})
    super
    # The @default_options hash contains merged options from AbstractStore::DEFAULT_OPTIONS
    # and any options passed to the constructor.
    @key = @default_options[:key]
  end

  private

  # Retrieves the session from the cookie.
  #
  # @param env [Hash] The Rack environment.
  # @param sid [String, nil] The session ID (can be nil for new sessions).
  # @return [Array<String, Hash>] An array containing the session ID and the session hash.
  def get_session(env, sid)
    request = Rack::Request.new(env)
    session_data = {}
    session_id = generate_sid # Start with a new SID, in case the cookie is invalid or absent

    if cookie_data = request.cookies[@key]
      begin
        marshaled_data = Base64.decode64(cookie_data)
        loaded_data = Marshal.load(marshaled_data)
        
        if loaded_data.is_a?(Hash)
          session_data = loaded_data
          # Extract session_id if embedded. If not, the initially generated one will be used.
          session_id = session_data.delete(:session_id) || session_id
        else
          # Handle cases where the loaded data isn't a hash (e.g., malformed or tampered)
          Rails.logger.warn "MarshalCookieStore: Loaded session data is not a Hash. Starting new session." if defined?(Rails)
          session_data = {}
          session_id = generate_sid
        end
      rescue EOFError, ArgumentError, TypeError => e
        # Corrupted cookie, treat as a new session
        Rails.logger.warn "MarshalCookieStore: Failed to load session from cookie: #{e.message}. Starting a new session." if defined?(Rails)
        session_data = {}
        session_id = generate_sid
      end
    end

    [session_id, session_data]
  end

  # Stores the session data in a cookie.
  #
  # @param env [Hash] The Rack environment.
  # @param sid [String] The session ID.
  # @param session_hash [Hash] The session data to store.
  # @param options [Hash] Options for setting the cookie.
  # @return [String] The session ID that was stored.
  def set_session(env, sid, session_hash, options)
    request = Rack::Request.new(env)
    cookie_jar = request.cookie_jar

    # Embed session_id into the hash for retrieval.
    # If `sid` is nil (e.g., a brand new session before first commit),
    # we generate a new one to ensure a stable ID is persisted.
    session_hash[:session_id] = sid || generate_sid

    marshaled_data = Marshal.dump(session_hash)
    encoded_data = Base64.encode64(marshaled_data)

    cookie_options = @default_options.merge(options)
    cookie_jar.write(@key, cookie_options.merge(value: encoded_data))

    # Return the session_id that was actually committed to the cookie.
    session_hash[:session_id]
  end

  # Destroys the session by deleting the session cookie.
  #
  # @param env [Hash] The Rack environment.
  # @param sid [String] The session ID of the session to destroy.
  # @param options [Hash] Options for deleting the cookie.
  # @return [String] A new session ID, indicating the start of a new (empty) session.
  def destroy_session(env, sid, options)
    request = Rack::Request.new(env)
    cookie_jar = request.cookie_jar

    cookie_options = @default_options.merge(options)
    cookie_jar.delete(@key, cookie_options)

    generate_sid # Return a new sid for the new session after destruction
  end

  # Generates a new unique session ID.
  #
  # @return [MarshalCookieStore::SessionId] A new session ID object.
  def generate_sid
    SessionId.new(SecureRandom.hex(32)) # Generate a 64-character hex string for robustness
  end
end
